claims.claim: FrameProcessorQueue is asyncio.PriorityQueue tagging SystemFrame HIGH_PRIORITY=1, else LOW_PRIORITY=2, with monotonic counters for FIFO within band
claims.found: class FrameProcessorQueue(asyncio.PriorityQueue): HIGH_PRIORITY=1; LOW_PRIORITY=2; __high_counter/__low_counter; put() checks isinstance(frame, SystemFrame)

claims.claim: Two tasks: input task processes SystemFrame inline, forwards non-system to __process_queue handled by separate task
claims.found: __input_frame_task_handler: if isinstance(frame, SystemFrame): await self.__process_frame(...); elif self.__process_queue: await self.__process_queue.put(...). Then __process_frame_task_handler handles process queue.
class InterruptionFrame(SystemFrame): 'Frame pushed to interrupt the pipeline.'
class UserStartedSpeakingFrame(SystemFrame): 'Frame indicating that the user turn has started.'

claims.claim: Semantic contract: DataFrames 'cancelled by user interruptions', SystemFrames 'not affected by user interruptions'
claims.found: SystemFrame docstring (l99): 'are not affected by user interruptions.' DataFrame docstring (l110-111): 'Data frames are cancelled by user interruptions.'

claims.claim: UninterruptibleFrame mixin marks DataFrames that must survive interruption (e.g. EndFrame, FunctionCallResultFrame)
claims.found: l136-147 UninterruptibleFrame mixin: 'data or control frames... preserved during interruptions'. l715 FunctionCallResultFrame(DataFrame, UninterruptibleFrame). NOTE: EndFrame is class EndFrame(ControlFrame, UninterruptibleFrame) at l1582 - a ControlFrame not DataFrame; the note's 'marks DataFrames' phrasing is slightly imprecise (mixin doc says 'data or control'), but the example list and cited mixin/715 lines are correct.

claims.claim: Vapi barge-in sequence must complete in under 100ms: VAD event -> abort LLM -> stop TTS -> clear buffers
claims.found: l49 'Interruption (barge-in) sequence — must complete in under 100ms:' l50 VAD detects speech start; l51 LLM request aborted; l52 TTS generation stops immediately; l53 Audio buffers cleared. (Actual file is vapi-pipeline-part-2.md; vapi-pipeline-2 is the source shortname.)
l649 on_user_started_speaking(): l650 cancel_active_action() l651 stop_tts_playback() l652 emit_state(“Listening”)

claims.claim: LiveKit has explicit interrupt() that cancels preemptive generation
claims.found: def interrupt(self, *, force=False) -> asyncio.Future[None]: 'Interrupt the current speech generation and any queued speeches.' calls self._cancel_preemptive_generation()

claims.claim: LiveKit on_end_of_turn is synchronous (non-cancellable) hook plus preemptive-generation cancellation
claims.found: def on_end_of_turn(...) -> bool: comment 'This method is sync to avoid it being cancelled by the AudioRecognition'; calls self._cancel_preemptive_generation()

claims.claim: Deepgram/orchestration treats user speech as higher-priority signal than pending actions (mechanism framing)
claims.found: l646 'this means treating user speech as a higher-priority signal than pending actions' - consistent with note's priority framing

summary: All 11 checkable assertions verified against ground truth. Two pipecat code_refs (119, 996) accurately describe the priority-queue + two-task mechanism. All frames.py citations (96-112, 136-147, 715, 951, 962) match. Source figures (Vapi <100ms barge-in sequence, Deepgram on_user_started_speaking) match exactly. LiveKit divergence citations (1268, 1921-1930) accurately describe sync on_end_of_turn + interrupt() + preemptive cancellation. Only nit: note calls EndFrame a 'DataFrame' in the UninterruptibleFrame example, but EndFrame is a ControlFrame (frames.py:1582); the mixin doc itself says 'data or control frames', so this is a minor imprecision, not a wrong citation. No WRONG findings.
